visuals.title: Lecture Note: Routh Examples 3 and 4
visuals.caption: The lesson starts with two fourth-order polynomials and their Routh arrays to expose the special boundary case.

visuals.title: Why Epsilon Matters in the First Column
visuals.description: A tiny epsilon replacement can flip the sign behavior of the later first-column entry and reveal a delicate boundary case.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Condition for jω-Axis Poles
visuals.caption: The note states the special rule involving a zero in the row indexed by s^1 while the other first-column entries remain positive.

visuals.title: Pole Pair on the jω Axis
visuals.description: The worked example identifies a purely imaginary pair at ±j√2.
